steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Text Structure and Purpose question. The question asks for the "overall structure" of the passage — meaning you must track how the passage moves from one idea to the next and match that movement to the correct description.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It identifies an issue concerning Matabele ants and then proposes a solution to address the issue.': The passage does not identify a problem that needs solving. Discovering that ants treat wounds is a finding, not an issue. No solution is proposed by the researchers. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It summarizes research findings on Matabele ants and then identifies an area for further research.': The first part of the passage reports the team's findings (ants treat infected wounds with antimicrobial secretions; mortality reduced by 90%). The final clause — "hopeful that this discovery could aid in the development of new antibiotics for human use" — points toward a future research direction. This matches perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It describes unique properties of Matabele ants and then speculates on how those properties evolved.': The passage never discusses how the ants' abilities evolved. There is no evolutionary speculation. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It introduces a study of Matabele ants and then explains the research methods used in the study.': The passage reports what the team found, not how they conducted the study. No methods are described. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
